config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society’s interests are important, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse and oppression. In my view, rights are the guardrails that keep society fair, especially for minorities and unpopular people. A healthy society should protect individuals first, then balance those rights with the common good when there is a real and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu adalah dasar yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh negara atau mayoritas, dan sejarah kita penuh dengan contoh ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hanya sah jika ada alasan yang jelas, sempit, dan proporsional, bukan sebagai cek kosong untuk mengorbankan kebebasan orang. Jadi bagi saya, hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itulah syarat agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak menindas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement as a general rule, because putting society first too easily becomes a blank check for the majority or the state to trample people who are inconvenient, different, or powerless. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “common interest” can be used to pressure individuals into silence, conformity, or unfair treatment, so I do not trust that idea unless it is tightly limited and justified. Society does matter, but it should be built on protecting individual rights first, then balancing them only when there is a clear and proportional public need. Without that, “society’s interests” can become just another name for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas yang harus menahan negara dan mayoritas agar tidak semena-mena. Poin tentang “kepentingan masyarakat” memang terdengar masuk akal, tetapi terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam minoritas atau mengorbankan kebebasan orang yang tidak populer. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang otomatis menempatkan kolektif di atas individu, melainkan yang hanya membatasi hak individu ketika ada kebutuhan publik yang sangat jelas, sempit, dan benar-benar proporsional. Kalau tidak ada batas seperti itu, “demi masyarakat” bisa berubah menjadi pembenaran yang rapi untuk penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general principle, because in Indonesia I have seen how “for the sake of society” can become a convenient excuse for social pressure, censorship, and abuse by authorities or the majority. Public interests do matter, but they should only override individual rights in narrow, clearly justified cases such as real harm, not as a default rule. The recent points about protecting minorities and limiting state power are exactly why I do not trust this statement when it is stated broadly. A fair society is not one that sacrifices individuals first; it is one that protects rights and then balances them carefully when there is a genuine public need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari perspektif saya di Amerika, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan negara atau mayoritas atas orang yang lemah atau tidak populer. Poin tentang perlunya kepentingan publik memang valid, tetapi kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi kartu bebas untuk mengorbankan hak dasar orang tanpa alasan yang sangat jelas, sempit, dan proporsional. Bagi saya, hak individu justru menjadi fondasi agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik, yang sering terjadi bukan keteraturan, melainkan penyalahgunaan yang dibungkus sebagai “demi kebaikan bersama.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
